_comment: Macro-Terrain: Azure Dragon Spine Sea. Per scale directive: this terrain IS a being. Player shouldn't initially realize they're standing on bone. Divine Sense -> Perception changes -> 'You realize this mountain range... is one finger.' Prime Directive: the being's corpse is objectively terrain; perception tier determines what the player sees.
name: Azure Dragon Spine Sea
scale: 1000 li long — the spine of a divine dragon.
canonical_event: The Azure Dragon's corpse formed this sea-ridge in the ancient era.

perception_tiered_appearance.mortal: A sea ridge 1000 li long, peaks break the surface as islands, stone is azure jade.
perception_tiered_appearance.qi_condensation: The sea currents swirl around the ridge in dragon-like patterns.
perception_tiered_appearance.foundation_establishment: The ridge is a petrified dragon spine; the islands are vertebrae.
perception_tiered_appearance.core_formation: This is the Azure Dragon's spine; the sea is its grave.
perception_tiered_appearance.nascent_soul: The dragon's water-essence still permeates the sea; cultivating here grants dragon-essence.
perception_tiered_appearance.spirit_severing: The Azure Dragon's soul sleeps in the deep; waking it would reshape the sea.

perception_mechanic.trigger: When player's perception tier rises, the appearance re-rolls automatically.
perception_mechanic.no_locked_upgrades: A mortal who later reaches Core Formation will see the truth; the terrain does not change, only understanding does.
perception_mechanic.canon_fidelity: This is exactly how Wang Lin perceived Ancient God Tu Si's remains — first as terrain, then as god-bone, then as god-memory.
